|---|------|---------|---------------|
| 1 | bash | git init | Initialize the git repository for the investment memo project |
| 2 | bash | mkdir -p | Create the required directory structure |
| 3 | bash | pip install | Install Python dependencies for data fetching and analysis |

| 5 | bash | python yfinance fetch | Fetch financial data for candidate companies |
| 6 | bash | curl SEC EDGAR | Download 10-K filings for selected company |
| 7 | bash | curl earnings transcripts | Download earnings call transcripts |
| 8 | bash | python extraction scripts | Parse and clean data from filings |
| 9 | bash | python financial model | Build three-statement financial model |
| 10 | bash | python memo generation | Generate the final investment memo |

EOF

cd /workspace/investment-memo && python3 << 'PYEOF'
import requests
import json
import hashlib
import os
from datetime import datetime

# GitLab's CIK number
CIK = "0001837200"
BASE_URL = f"https://efts.sec.gov/LATEST/search-index?q=exhibit_number:10-K&entity_type:all&company_name:GitLab&date_range:auto&output=atom"

# First, let's get the latest 10-K filing URL
def get_filings(cik, form_type="10-K"):
    """Get filing URLs from EDGAR's XML index"""
    url = f"https://data.sec.gov/submissions/CIK{cik.zfill(10)}.json"
    resp = requests.get(url, timeout=30)
    if resp.status_code != 200:
        print(f"Error fetching index: {resp.status_code}")
        return []
    
    data = resp.json()
    filings = data.get("filings", {})
    recent = filings.get("recent", {})
    form_list = recent.get("form", [])
    acc_nums = recent.get("accessionNumber", [])
    
    results = []
    for form, acc in zip(form_list, acc_nums):
        if form == form_type:
            # Clean accession number
            clean_acc = acc.replace("-", "")
            results.append({
                "form": form,
                "accessionNumber": acc,
                "cik": cik,
                "filingDate": recent.get("filingDate", [])[form_list.index(form)] if form in form_list else "",
            })
    return results

# Get all 10-K filings
print("Fetching 10-K filings index...")
filings = get_filings(CIK, "10-K")
print(f"Found {len(filings)} 10-K filings")

# Also get 10-Q filings
print("Fetching 10-Q filings index...")
q_filings = get_filings(CIK, "10-Q")
print(f"Found {len(q_filings)} 10-Q filings")

# Print what we found
for f in filings:
    print(f"  10-K: {f}")
for f in q_filings[:5]:
    print(f"  10-Q: {f}")

import requests
import json
import hashlib
import os
from datetime import datetime

CIK = "0001837200"

# Try the EDGAR company filings page with proper headers
headers = {
    'User-Agent': 'InvestmentMemoResearch/1.0 (research@example.com)',
    'Accept': 'application/json',
}

# Method 1: Try the XHR endpoint
url = f"https://data.sec.gov/submissions/CIK{CIK}.json"
resp = requests.get(url, headers=headers, timeout=30)
print(f"Method 1 (data.sec.gov): {resp.status_code}")
if resp.status_code == 200:
    data = resp.json()
    filings = data.get("filings", {}).get("recent", {})
    forms = filings.get("form", [])
    dates = filings.get("filingDate", [])
    accs = filings.get("accessionNumber", [])
    print(f"Found {len(forms)} recent filings")
    for i, (f, d, a) in enumerate(zip(forms, dates, accs)):
        if f in ["10-K", "10-Q"]:
            print(f"  {f} | {d} | {a}")
else:
    print(f"Response: {resp.text[:200]}")

import requests
import json
import hashlib
import os
from datetime import datetime

CIK = "0001837200"

headers = {
    'User-Agent': 'InvestmentMemoResearch/1.0 (research@example.com)',
}

# Use the EDGAR company filings page with JSON output
# This gives us all filings
url = f"https://www.sec.gov/cgi-bin/browse-edgar?action=getcompany&CIK={CIK}&type=10-K&dateb=&owner=include&count=100&search_text=&action=getcompany&output=xml"
resp = requests.get(url, headers=headers, timeout=30)
print(f"Company filings XML: {resp.status_code}")
if resp.status_code == 200:
    # Parse the XML to find 10-K URLs
    import xml.etree.ElementTree as ET
    root = ET.fromstring(resp.content)
    ns = {'sec': 'http://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data'}
    
    # Find all accession entries
    for item in root.findall('.//item'):
        print(f"Item: {item.attrib}")
    
    # Try different parsing
    text = resp.text[:2000]
    print(text[:1500])
else:
    print(resp.text[:500])

cd /workspace/investment-memo && python3 << 'PYEOF'
import requests
import json
import hashlib
import os
from datetime import datetime

headers = {'User-Agent': 'InvestmentMemoResearch/1.0 (research@example.com)'}

# Get all filings for GitLab
url = "https://efts.sec.gov/LATEST/search-index?q=GitLab&entity_type:all&date_range:auto&output=atom"
resp = requests.get(url, headers=headers, timeout=30)
data = resp.json()
hits = data.get('hits', {}).get('hits', [])

# Categorize filings
filings_by_type = {}
for h in hits:
    src = h.get('_source', {})
    forms = src.get('root_forms', [])
    for form in forms:
        if form not in filings_by_type:
            filings_by_type[form] = []
        filings_by_type[form].append({
            'form': form,
            'name': src.get('display_names', [''])[0],
            'primary_doc': src.get('primary_doc', ''),
            'filing_date': src.get('filing_date', ''),
            'report_date': src.get('report_date', ''),
            'accession_number': src.get('accession_number', ''),
            'document_url': src.get('document_url', ''),
        })

for form_type, items in sorted(filings_by_type.items()):
    print(f"\n{form_type}: {len(items)} filings")
    for item in items[:5]:
        print(f"  {item['filing_date']} | {item['primary_doc']} | {item['accession_number']}")
    if len(items) > 5:
        print(f"  ... and {len(items)-5} more")

PYEOF

import requests
import json
import hashlib
import os
from datetime import datetime

headers = {'User-Agent': 'InvestmentMemoResearch/1.0 (research@example.com)'}

# Use the EDGAR company filings API with the correct CIK
CIK = "0001653482"

# Get the full filings list
url = f"https://data.sec.gov/submissions/CIK{CIK}.json"
resp = requests.get(url, headers=headers, timeout=30)
data = resp.json()

print(f"Company: {data.get('name')}")
print(f"SIC: {data.get('sic')}")
print(f"Filings keys: {list(data.get('filings', {}).keys())}")

# Check the 'files' key which has all filings
files = data.get('filings', {}).get('files', [])
print(f"\nTotal files: {len(files)}")

# Filter for 10-K and 10-Q
for f in files:
    form = f.get('type', '')
    if form in ['10-K', '10-Q', '8-K', 'DEF 14A', '424B2']:
        print(f"  {form} | {f.get('date', '')} | {f.get('name', '')} | {f.get('size', '')}")

import requests
import json
import hashlib
import os
from datetime import datetime

headers = {'User-Agent': 'InvestmentMemoResearch/1.0 (research@example.com)'}

# Use the EDGAR full-text search API with better query
url = "https://efts.sec.gov/LATEST/search-index?q=company_name:GitLab+exhibit_number:10-K&entity_type:all&date_range:auto&output=atom"
resp = requests.get(url, headers=headers, timeout=30)
data = resp.json()
hits = data.get('hits', {}).get('hits', [])
print(f"10-K results: {len(hits)}")
for h in hits:
    src = h.get('_source', {})
    print(f"  Doc: {src.get('primary_doc', '')} | URL: {src.get('document_url', '')} | Date: {src.get('filing_date', '')}")

# Also try without exhibit_number filter
url2 = "https://efts.sec.gov/LATEST/search-index?q=company_name:GitLab&entity_type:all&date_range:auto&output=atom"
resp2 = requests.get(url2, headers=headers, timeout=30)
data2 = resp2.json()
hits2 = data2.get('hits', {}).get('hits', [])
print(f"\nAll GitLab results: {len(hits2)}")
for h in hits2[:20]:
    src = h.get('_source', {})
    forms = src.get('root_forms', [])
    print(f"  Forms: {forms} | Doc: {src.get('primary_doc', '')} | URL: {src.get('document_url', '')} | Date: {src.get('filing_date', '')}")
